name: Real: Batch evaluate 20 product images and flag low-quality ones
prompt:
An e-commerce team needs quality control. Write the TypeScript code that implements this request:

'I have 20 generated product images. I need a script that evaluates each one against the original prompt and gives it a score from 1 to 5 on: how well it matches the product, how good the lighting is, and whether the colors are accurate. Flag any image that scores below 4 on any dimension so we can regenerate them. Use a cheap vision model for scoring, not the expensive image-generation model.'

Your code should export a function `batchEvaluate(images: Array<{ base64: string; prompt: string }>): Promise<Array<{ id: number; scores: { match: number; lighting: number; color: number }; flagged: boolean }>>` that evaluates all images. Do NOT write a main() runner.

assertions.name: no_image_config
assertions.check: does NOT use imageConfig or responseModalities in eval call
assertions.test: (c) => { const evalCall = c.match(/evaluate|judge|score/i) ? c.match(/generateContent[\s\S]{0,300}/i)?.[0] ?? c : c; return !(/imageConfig/.test(evalCall) && /responseModalities/.test(evalCall)); }
